Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Deposition Questions; And For Monetary Sanctions In Having To File The Motion To Compel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Jul-06-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court releases Mandarin Court Interpreter Mimi Lain. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF IBARRA PROFESSIONAL LAW CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Defendant To Answer Deposition Questions; And For Monetary Sanctions In Having To File The Motion To Compel.

Motion denied as untimely since deadlines for discovery and motions concerning discovery passed long ago, and there is no pending motion by any party to reopen / extend discovery motion deadline. Pelton-Shepherd Industries, Inc. v. Delta Packaging Products, Inc., 165 Cal. App. 4th 1568 (2008).

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at williamclynn@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: William Lynn, Clerk:M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)
